metadata.title: P5-005c Draft Model Acquisition v2: Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B (INT8 OpenVINO IR)
metadata.prior_attempt: P5-005c v1 (commit 6b0e7e5) — ACQUISITION_FAILED. Root cause: script searched for INT4 models and rejected the only available repo (INT8_ASYM). All conversion strategies failed because the model is only published as a pre-converted OpenVINO IR — no PyTorch source weights exist.
metadata.scope: Download the pre-built OpenVINO IR model from HuggingFace, validate architecture compatibility, run standalone and speculative-decoding smoke tests on Arc 140V GPU. No conversion or quantization needed — the model is already in OpenVINO format.
metadata.exit_deliverable: A fully validated model directory at models/qwen3-0.6b-pruned-6l/openvino-int8-gpu/ with passing GPU smoke test and speculative-decoding compatibility confirmation against Qwen3-14B, plus an updated JSON evidence artifact.

execution_prompt.role: You are a single-session Execution Agent. Execute exactly the milestone defined below — no more, no less. Do NOT generate SDO prompts, continuation prompts, or offer to plan future milestones. Stop after milestone completion.
execution_prompt.critical_correction:
THE PREVIOUS ATTEMPT FAILED BECAUSE OF INCORRECT ASSUMPTIONS. Here is what is now confirmed via web search:
1. EXACT REPO ID: OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov - Published by Intel's OpenVINO team (NOT by Qwen) - This is the ONLY available version of this model
2. FORMAT: Already converted to OpenVINO IR format with INT8_ASYM weight compression via NNCF - NO CONVERSION NEEDED — just download with huggingface_hub.snapshot_download - Do NOT attempt optimum-cli export, Python API export, or any other conversion strategy - Do NOT reject the model for being INT8 instead of INT4 — INT8 is the correct format
3. ARCHITECTURE: Qwen3ForCausalLM with 22 hidden layers (not 6) - "6L" in the name means 6 layers were REMOVED from the 28-layer Qwen3-0.6B, leaving 22 - hidden_size=1024, vocab_size=151936 — same as full Qwen3-0.6B - The model was fine-tuned against Qwen3-8B but is architecturally compatible with Qwen3-14B because speculative decoding only requires matching vocab_size
4. COMPATIBILITY: OpenVINO 2025.2+ and Optimum Intel 1.25.3+ (our runtime is 2026.0 — compatible)
5. SPECULATIVE DECODING API: The model card shows this exact usage pattern: ```python import openvino_genai draft_model = openvino_genai.draft_model(draft_model_path, draft_device) scheduler_config = openvino_genai.SchedulerConfig() scheduler_config.cache_size = 2 pipe = openvino_genai.LLMPipeline(main_model_path, main_device, scheduler_config=scheduler_config, draft_model=draft_model) ``` HOWEVER: P5-005b used a DIFFERENT API pattern (without SchedulerConfig): ```python config = {"draft_model": ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_model_path), "GPU")} pipe = ov_genai.LLMPipeline(str(model_dir), "GPU", config) ``` Try the P5-005b pattern FIRST (for consistency). If it fails, try the model-card pattern with SchedulerConfig.
6. NO PYTORCH WEIGHTS EXIST: The repo only contains openvino_model.xml, openvino_model.bin, and config files. Strategies B-E from the previous prompt are IMPOSSIBLE. Do not attempt them.

context.hardware: Intel Core Ultra 7 258V (Lunar Lake), Arc 140V iGPU (~16GB shared LPDDR5X), 32GB total (31.323GB effective per ADR-005). Windows 11 Pro host. PowerShell terminal. Python .venv at .venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1.
context.existing_models: - models/qwen3-14b/openvino-int4-gpu/ — Target model (Qwen3ForCausalLM, 40 layers, hidden_size=5120, vocab_size=151936) - models/qwen3-0.6b/openvino-int4-gpu/ — Current draft model (Qwen3ForCausalLM, 28 layers, hidden_size=1024, vocab_size=151936, ~367MB bin)
context.target_model: HuggingFace repo: OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov Architecture: Qwen3ForCausalLM, 22 layers, hidden_size=1024, vocab_size=151936 Quantization: INT8_ASYM (NNCF compressed) Target local directory: models/qwen3-0.6b-pruned-6l/openvino-int8-gpu/ NOTE: Directory name uses "int8" (not "int4") because the model IS int8.
context.prior_art: The P5-005b benchmark harness at phase2_gates/scripts/run_p5_feasibility_005b.py contains the proven speculative decoding pipeline pattern (see create_pipeline function around line 385). The acquisition script at phase2_gates/scripts/acquire_p5_005a_models.py contains proven patterns for: - OpenVINO tokenizer asset generation (ensure_openvino_tokenizer_assets) - Model integrity snapshots (SHA-256, file presence, size validation) - GPU smoke test (quick_inference_smoke) - AC power enforcement, disk space checks, atomic JSON evidence writing Reuse these patterns where applicable.

step.name: Branch and Environment Setup
step.action: Switch to branch feature/p5-005c-pruned-draft-acquisition (already exists from v1 attempt).
Activate .venv. Verify openvino, openvino_genai, huggingface_hub imports succeed.
Verify AC power is connected (psutil.sensors_battery).
Verify disk space >= 2GB free on C:\ (the model is ~300-400MB; generous buffer).

step.name: Direct Download from HuggingFace
step.action: Download the model using the EXACT repo ID: ```python import huggingface_hub huggingface_hub.snapshot_download( repo_id="OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov", local_dir=str(ROOT / "models" / "qwen3-0.6b-pruned-6l" / "openvino-int8-gpu"), local_dir_use_symlinks=False, ) ```
Verify that openvino_model.xml and openvino_model.bin exist in the download directory.
If the download fails (network error, repo not found), record the error and set disposition ACQUISITION_FAILED.

step.name: Architecture Validation
step.action: Read config.json from the downloaded directory and verify: - "architectures" contains "Qwen3ForCausalLM" - "vocab_size" == 151936 (MUST match Qwen3-14B for speculative decoding) - "num_hidden_layers" == 22 (expected for the 6-layer-pruned variant) - "hidden_size" == 1024
Record full config.json contents in the evidence artifact.
If vocab_size does NOT equal 151936, STOP — set disposition INCOMPATIBLE.

step.name: Tokenizer Asset Validation
step.action: Check if the downloaded directory contains openvino_tokenizer.xml, openvino_tokenizer.bin, openvino_detokenizer.xml, openvino_detokenizer.bin.

If these files are MISSING: generate them using openvino_tokenizers.convert_tokenizer, loading the tokenizer from the downloaded directory (it should contain tokenizer.json or tokenizer.model). If the tokenizer files are not in the download either, COPY them from models/qwen3-0.6b/openvino-int4-gpu/ (same tokenizer family, same vocab_size=151936).
Record which method was used to obtain the tokenizer assets.

step.name: Standalone GPU Smoke Test
step.action: Load the pruned model standalone on GPU: ```python pipe = ov_genai.LLMPipeline(str(model_dir), "GPU") config = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() config.max_new_tokens = 20 config.do_sample = False output = pipe.generate("What is 2+2?", config) ```
Record: success/fail, total latency_ms, output_text_preview, RSS after load.
Delete the pipeline and gc.collect() after the test to free GPU memory for the next step.

step.name: Speculative Decoding Compatibility Smoke Test
step.description: THE CRITICAL GATE. Verify the pruned INT8 model works as a draft for the Qwen3-14B INT4 target model.
step.action: ATTEMPT 1 — P5-005b API pattern (preferred for benchmark consistency): ```python import openvino_genai as ov_genai draft = ov_genai.draft_model( str(ROOT / "models" / "qwen3-0.6b-pruned-6l" / "openvino-int8-gpu"), "GPU" ) pipe = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(ROOT / "models" / "qwen3-14b" / "openvino-int4-gpu"), "GPU", {"draft_model": draft} ) gen_config = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() gen_config.max_new_tokens = 50 gen_config.num_assistant_tokens = 3 gen_config.do_sample = False output = pipe.generate("Write a Python function that adds two numbers.", gen_config) ```

If ATTEMPT 1 fails, try ATTEMPT 2 — model-card SchedulerConfig pattern: ```python draft = ov_genai.draft_model( str(ROOT / "models" / "qwen3-0.6b-pruned-6l" / "openvino-int8-gpu"), "GPU" ) scheduler_config = ov_genai.SchedulerConfig() scheduler_config.cache_size = 2 pipe = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(ROOT / "models" / "qwen3-14b" / "openvino-int4-gpu"), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler_config, draft_model=draft ) gen_config = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() gen_config.max_new_tokens = 50 gen_config.num_assistant_tokens = 3 gen_config.do_sample = False output = pipe.generate("Write a Python function that adds two numbers.", gen_config) ```

Record for whichever attempt succeeds: - API pattern used (P5-005b or SchedulerConfig) - success/fail - output text preview - approximate decode TPS (time the generate call, divide max_new_tokens by elapsed) - RSS after pipeline creation

If BOTH attempts fail, record FULL error tracebacks for both and set disposition STANDALONE_ONLY. Common failure modes to capture: - Shape mismatch errors (vocab_size incompatibility) - Draft model compilation errors on GPU - Memory allocation failures - "Unsupported draft model" type errors

step.name: Model Integrity Snapshot
step.action: Compute SHA-256 hashes of openvino_model.bin and openvino_model.xml.
Record all file names and sizes in the downloaded directory.

Compare bin file size against the existing 0.6B model (~367MB). The pruned model should be ~22/28 = ~79% of the size (~290MB expected) since it has 22 of 28 layers, though INT8 quantization (vs INT4) may make it larger per layer.

step.name: Evidence Artifact and Commit
step.action: Write the complete evidence artifact to: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_005c_pruned_draft_acquisition.json (This OVERWRITES the v1 failed evidence artifact — that's intentional.)

The artifact MUST contain: - metadata: timestamp, git commit, runtime versions, AC power state, disk space - model_source: exact HF repo ID ("OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov"), quantization format ("INT8_ASYM") - architecture_validation: config.json contents, vocab_size match confirmation, num_hidden_layers - download: elapsed time, files downloaded, any errors - tokenizer: method used (downloaded / generated / copied from 0.6b) - standalone_smoke: GPU inference result (success/fail, latency, output, RSS) - speculative_smoke: Qwen3-14B + pruned draft result (success/fail, API pattern used, latency, TPS, output, RSS) - comparison_vs_06b: file size comparison, layer count comparison (22 vs 28) - disposition: one of: - READY_FOR_BENCHMARK — both smoke tests passed, model is fully prepared - STANDALONE_ONLY — standalone works but speculative decoding with 14B failed (record why) - ACQUISITION_FAILED — download failed - INCOMPATIBLE — architecture/vocab mismatch

Git add and commit with message: P5-005c: Acquire Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov draft model (v2)
Milestone: P5-005c-ACQUIRE v2 (direct OpenVINO IR download) Source: OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov (INT8_ASYM, 22 layers) Disposition: {DISPOSITION} Evidence: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_005c_pruned_draft_acquisition.json
v1 failed due to incorrect assumption that INT4 was required and that conversion from source weights was possible. v2 downloads the pre-built OpenVINO IR directly.

error_handling.known_issue: The v1 script rejected OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov because it was INT8 not INT4. CORRECTION: INT8 is the ONLY available quantization and is perfectly valid for a draft model. Do NOT filter or reject based on quantization format in v2.

This model repo contains ONLY OpenVINO IR files (openvino_model.xml, openvino_model.bin) and config/tokenizer files. There are NO PyTorch weights (no pytorch_model.bin, no model.safetensors). Do NOT attempt any export or conversion — the model is already ready.

The target model (Qwen3-14B) is INT4 while the draft model is INT8. This is architecturally fine — speculative decoding only requires matching vocab_size for the logit comparison. The internal quantization of each model is independent. If OpenVINO GenAI raises an error about mixed quantization, record the exact error message — this would be a runtime limitation, not an architectural incompatibility.

The OpenVINO-published repo may not include openvino_tokenizer.xml/bin and openvino_detokenizer.xml/bin. It should include tokenizer.json and/or tokenizer.model. If the OpenVINO tokenizer assets are missing, either generate them with openvino_tokenizers.convert_tokenizer OR copy them from the existing models/qwen3-0.6b/openvino-int4-gpu/ directory (identical tokenizer, same vocab_size=151936).

Two API patterns exist for speculative decoding in OpenVINO GenAI: 1. Dict-config pattern (used in P5-005b): config={"draft_model": ov_genai.draft_model(...)} 2. SchedulerConfig pattern (shown in model card): scheduler_config=..., draft_model=... Try pattern 1 first. If it fails, try pattern 2. Record which worked.
error_handling.general_principle: This is a SIMPLE DOWNLOAD + VALIDATE task. Do not over-engineer it. The model is already in the correct format. Download it, check files, run two smoke tests, record evidence, commit.

constraints.constraint: Do NOT modify any existing model directories (qwen3-14b, qwen3-0.6b, etc.).
Do NOT modify any production code in services/, shared/, or launcher/.
Do NOT run any benchmark — this milestone is acquisition and validation only.
Do NOT modify docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md — the SDO will update it after review.
Do NOT attempt model conversion, quantization, or export — the model is already in OpenVINO IR format.
Do NOT reject the model for being INT8 instead of INT4.
AC power MUST be connected before starting.

execution_prompt.initialization_instruction: When starting the Execution Agent session: 1. Attach the 5 files listed in required_attachments. 2. Paste this entire XML prompt as the first message. 3. The agent should begin with Step 1 (branch switch and environment verification). 4. This is a v2 retry — the branch already exists. Do not create a new branch.
